heading: Embezzlement against estate
label: 153 Embezzlement against estate
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Bankruptcy (, ; (part), ; (part), ).section 52(a) of title 11July 1, 1898, ch. 541, § 29a30 Stat. 554May 27, 1926, ch. 406, § 1144 Stat. 665June 22, 1938, ch. 575, § 152 Stat. 855Minor changes were made in phraseology.

Amendments1996—Subsec. (a). substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $5,000”.Pub. L. 104–2941994— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “Whoever knowingly and fraudulently appropriates to his own use, embezzles, spends, or transfers any property or secretes or destroys any document belonging to the estate of a debtor which came into his charge as trustee, custodian, marshal, or other officer of the court, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than five years, or both.”Pub. L. 103–394substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $5,000”.Pub. L. 103–3221978— struck out “, receiver” after “trustee” in section catchline and in text struck out “receiver,” before “custodian” and substituted “debtor” for “bankrupt”.Pub. L. 95–598

children.heading: .—Offense
children.label: a .—Offense
children.text: A person described in subsection (b) who knowingly and fraudulently appropriates to the person’s own use, embezzles, spends, or transfers any property or secretes or destroys any document belonging to the estate of a debtor shall be fined under this title, imprisoned not more than 5 years, or both.

children.heading: .—Person to Whom Section Applies
children.label: b .—Person to Whom Section Applies
children.text: A person described in this subsection is one who has access to property or documents belonging to an estate by virtue of the person’s participation in the administration of the estate as a trustee, custodian, marshal, attorney, or other officer of the court or as an agent, employee, or other person engaged by such an officer to perform a service with respect to the estate.
